calendar.matters:
Petition Of Special Administrator For Relief From Judgment Of Final Distributio;Setting First And Final Account And Report Of Executor; For Allowance Of Statutory Executor'S Commissions And Attorney'S Fees; For Allowance Of Attorney'S Extraordinary Fees; And Fro Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced; Memorandum In Suppoert Of Mition For Relief, And Petition Of Formal Special Administrator For Statutory Compensation, And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Shelton in Propria Persona. Harold Mangar in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we are here for Mr. Shelton's request for relief from judgment and for payment of fees, that there are concerns, that he did not appear at the hearing, that the parties who were present did not request a continuance, that there is nothing excusable in not representing your interests, that the appointment was on the basis that there might be a problem, that there was no agreement that the estate would be charged, that no problems were found and that the court does not see why Mr. Mangar should pay. Mr. Shelton apologized for not appearing on 6/9/15 and that he did not know that the order for final distribution would be signed. The Court denied the petition of special administrator for relief from judgment of final distribution, etc., and stated that Mr. Mangar can submit an order if he would like.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition Of Special Administrator For Relief From Judgment Of Final Distributio;Setting First And Final Account And Report Of Executor; For Allowance Of Statutory Executor'S Commissions And Attorney'S Fees; For Allowance Of Attorney'S Extraordinary Fees; And Fro Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced; Memorandum In Suppoert Of Mition For Relief, And Petition Of Formal Special Administrator For Statutory Compensation, And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-24-2015 Continued To Sep-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dr. Harold Mangar in Propria Persona. Joshua Henderson (via court call) for Katherine Lagenour. Ryan Szczepanik (via court call) for the Charities.

The Court stated that the objections have been withdrawn and asked if there was anything left the two petitions. Mr. Szczepanik stated that it was the charities position that the court should proceed with its ruling. Mr. Henderson stated that with respect to the removal petition they are still requesting that relief and that they are ready for the court to rule. The court stated that it was ready to deny the petition to remove and to grant the accounting petition after making the appropriate determinations regarding fees. Mr. Henderson expressed his concern regarding Mr. Manger stating he would not make distribution and that there was an agreement regarding fees paid toward getting information. The Court stated that the order would order the distribution and that any side agreements were not before the court. Mr. Szczepanik stated that Peter Shelton (special administrator) is looking for guidance regarding his fees, asked if there was anything special he needed to do and suggested that the statutory fees could be allocated between Mr. Shelton & Mr. Mangar. The Court responded that there was nothing before the court regarding Mr. Shelton's fees and asked if the parties wanted a continuance to a date that Mr. Shelton could be present. The petition to remove Harold Mangar as executor was denied by the court. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Harold Mangar As Executor And Appoint A Special Administrator
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Denied

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To Jun-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Harold Mangar As Executor And Appoint A Special Administrator
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To Jun-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Continued To Apr-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Harold Mangar (executor) in Propria Persona. Ryan Szczepanik for the 3 Charities. Josh Henderson for Trina Lagenour.

The court stated that the charities have concerns that Dr. Mangar has not looked for additional accounts and that they want Dr. Mangar suspended of his duties and to appoint Mr. Shelton as Special Administrator. Dr. Manger reported that he has called the investment firms, is told the accounts are closed and states that he needs the account numbers from Ms. Lagenour to obtain the information. Mr. Henderson stated that they do not have the account numbers and that Mr. Mangar has done nothing to marshal the accounts. Mr. Szczepanik stated that the charities request removal or suspension, that it has taken Mr. Mangar over 15 months to follow up on the accounts, that today is the first time Mr. Mangar has acknowledged reaching out to an investment company, that Dr. Mangar sold the automobile and paid his son over 27% of the sale, that Dr. Mangar has not produced the US Bank records and that an independant individual is needed to handle the estate. Dr. Mangar reported that he used the proceeds of the sale of the auto to pay funeral expenses and that his attorney spent 2 hours with a real estate agent taking photos and compiling an inventory. Mr. Szczepanik spoke of the sufficiency of compensation, the preliminary distribution being granted and the distribution of funds. The court stated that it is not prepared to remove Dr. Mangar absent concern regarding the additional assets, that it will suspend Dr. Mangar and appoint Mr. Shelton as temporary special administrator with letters being issued accordingly, requested that Dr. Mangar cooperate and Mr. Sczcepanik submit a form of order. The accounting petition and the petition to remove Harold Mangar as executor and appoint a special administrator were continued to April 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, and the suspension and special administrator appointment are to run until this date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Harold Mangar As Executor And Appoint A Special Administrator
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Continued To Apr-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2015 Continued To Feb-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Szczepanik for KQED, S.F. Opera and The Metropolitan Opera. Harold Mangar in Propria Persona.

Mr. Henderson stated that they have served Mr. Mangar with the petition several times and that he believes they have filed something with regard to service. Mr. Mangar stated that he has not received anything, that he has an objection, that they can get passed the service issue and addressed issues/allegations raised in the petition. Mr. Henderson stated that he believes the check to Ms. Lagenour has been cashed. Mr. Szczepanik stated that his position is the Court should first address the petition for removal, appointment of a special administrator and then the final account and fees. Mr. Szczepanik also stated that the Charities believe the beginning balance on hand is most likely inaccurate and referred to Mr. Mangar's testimony. The Court explained to Mr. Mangar that original bank statements need to be filed or it can not proceed with the accounting. The petition for removal, etc. and the accounting petition were continued to February 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to give Mr. Mangar the opportunity to file appropriate pleadings, objections, request for fees and or petitions to bring the merits before the Court. Mr. Mangar is to file his documents if any as well as the original bank statements by February 3, 2015. Responses/replies are to be filed by February 17, 2015. The Court expects during that time that the parties talk to each other and provide information. The Court also stated that it would be helpful for Mr. Mangar to have an attorney.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Receipts On Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-16-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Shaver appeared by telephone for the Charities. Harold Mangar in Propria Persona.

Mr. Mangar stated that he received receipts from the Charities, that he filed them on Monday, that the Attorney for Ms. Lagenour refused to sign the receipt and that he has sent the Attorney six different forms to sign. Mr. Shaver stated that they are satisfied with distribution at this time. Status on filing of receipts on distribution was taken off calendar. The Court informed Mr. Mangar that a petition for final distribution is now needed and that it will require an accounting. Mr. Mangar stated that he has an Attorney that will help him. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution was set for December 16, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. (Not Reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Josh Henderson for and with Katherine Lagenour. Ryan Szczepanik for KQED, The San Francisco Opera and The Metropolitan Opera. Harold Mangar in Propria Persona.

Mr. Henderson stated that his Client wanted to meet with all of the other Beneficiaries to discuss all of the issues and see if they can come to an agreement, that several things are at issue and that it is beneficial to get these things resolved. The Court stated that Ms. Lagenour only has a claim to the personal property and asked why should she hold up distribution to the other Beneficiaries. Mr. Henderson stated that his Client would like to pursue elder abuse with the other Beneficiaries. Mr. Szczepanik stated that the Charities are willing to allocate $11,000.00 for the automobile and $5,000.00 for the cash gift to Ms. Lagenour, that enough funds will remain for fees, that there is more than sufficient funds to distribute 80%, that his Clients are prejudiced against and that there is no reason to delay any further. The Court raised the issue of no notice on the petition. Mr. Henderson stated that he intends to move forward on the petition to remove Executor, etc. Mr. Szcepanik stated that the Charities' position is that they received notice and will not object. Mr. Mangar stated that he wants proper notice and expressed that he is not responsible for investments made by the Decedent. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution was taken under submission. The Petition to Remove Harold Mangar as Executor, etc. was continued to September 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Status on filing of receipt of blocked account was continued to August 12, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Final Inventory
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Off Calendar

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Continued To Aug-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Szczepanik for KQED, San Francisco Opera and Metropolitan Opera, Charities. Josh Henderson for (Objector) Katherine Lagenour. Dr. Harold Mangar (executor) in Propria Persona.

The court addressed the discharge of Dr. Mangar's attorney and his ability to handle this on his own in light of his fiduciary duty, and asked where the escrow funds were being held. Mr. Szczepanik indicated that the funds are in an UMPQUA account in the amount of $102,000.00, and that the assets are being depleted by the parties with all of the fighting, which will limit the amount of money that will go to the charities. Mr. Henderson indicated that there are items missing from the home, the vehicle has been sold and that the safe door has been broken off. The court stated that there is a petition on file to remove Dr. Mangar as executor, but did not think that Katherine Lagenour was the right choice, and that it had no objections to a preliminary distribution. The court ordered Dr. Mangar to place the funds into a blocked account. Status on filing of the receipt was set for August 5, 2014. The court will not allow Dr. Mangar to continue to represent himself. The court also stated it would like to see a special administrator appointed and that preliminary distribution is in order. The petition for preliminary distribution was continued to August 12, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition to remove Dr. Mangar on calendar for August 11, 2014 is also continued to August 12, 2014. The court will issue an order placing funds into a blocked account.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Continued To Jul-29-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For An Order To Issue Citation
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Judith Baer for Harold Mangar. Josh Henderson for Katerine Lagenour (Objector).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Ms. Lagenour has standing to take the deposition of Dr. Mangar, whether or not Ms. Lagenour violated the "no contest clause", whether Ms. Lagenour is an interested person pursuant to probate code, whether Ms. Lagenour's beneficial interest will be effected by an elder abuse action, the value of the missing ring effecting Ms. Lagenour's interest, whether or not the deposition should be restricted to the issue of the missing ring and the car, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: It is hereby ordered that Objector, Katerine Valencia Lagenour's, Notice of Motion and Motion for A Petition for a Citation for Harold B. Mangar to Appear before the Court and be Examined under oath pursuant to Probate Code 8870, or Alternatively, to Appear for Depostion is hereby granted.

It is hereby ordered that Harold B. Mangar appear for deposition at the offices of Carcione, Catermole, Dolinski, et al, located at 601 Brewster Avenue, Redwood City, California, on a date and time to be arranged by the parties. (Order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Court Reporter: Maya Morohoshi, CSR 564 (650) 712-1059. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (Reported)

TRIAL DISPOSITION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Petition For A Citation For Harold B. Mangar To Appear Before The Court And Be Examined Under Oath Pursuant To Probate Code Section 8870, Or Alternatively, To Appear For Deposition
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Katherine Valencia Lagenour'S Ex Parte Application For An Order Shortening Time To Hear Petn. For A Citation For Harold B. Mangar To Appear Before The Court And Be Examined Under Oath
Off Calendar Oct-18-2013 Denied

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Judith Baer for Harold Mangar (Petitioner). Joe Carcione for and with Katherine Valencia Lagenour (Objector). Earl Blauner from KQED. Mark Jones from San Francisco Opera. Kilby Stenkamp (Listing Agent).

Mr. Carcione presented the Court with an verification to the objections. He stated the reason for the objections, that the word "automobiles" is missing from the Will, that there may have a cause of action against Harold Mangar for funds from a reverse mortgage, that he wants to take Mr. Mangar's deposition to find out what happen to the money. Ms. Baer stated the decedent's house needs to be sold soon to avoid foreclosure. Mr. Carciione stated that he does not object to the sale of the house and distribution to the charities but he expressed concern that estate funds will be use for litigation. Discussions were held with regard to how to proceed. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary with full authority (order for the same signe in open court). No bond is required. Mr. Carcione stated that he wants to submit an order after hearing to include taking deposition of Mr. Mangar, etc. The Court indicated that he will have to file a petition for such order. Ms. Baer requested to have Ms. Lagenour removed the household furniture and furnishings before the house is sold. Mr. Carcione agreed to move the household furniture and furnishings.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
